text.title: Two-Pass: Free-Form Reasoning Then Structured Extraction Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Author adds a 'reasoning' field to the strict schema and claims it equals two-pass.
antipattern.root-cause: Embedded scratchpad works for medium tasks; on hard tasks the grammar mask still degrades the reasoning trace.
antipattern.fix: Switch to two-pass when difficulty saturates the scratchpad — A/B is the test.

antipattern.symptom: Pass 2 uses Opus or another strong model with strict SO 'for safety', erasing the cost win.
antipattern.root-cause: Extraction does not need strong-model reasoning; defaulting to Opus is over-engineering.
antipattern.fix: Use Haiku (or equivalent) for pass 2. Strong-model extraction is only justified when Haiku fails a structured-output eval > 5%.

antipattern.symptom: Two-pass adopted on faith. Subsequent eval shows single-pass was within 2%, doubling cost for nothing.
antipattern.root-cause: Cargo-cult on the pattern without measuring task difficulty.
antipattern.fix: Run A/B on 50+ tasks before adopting; require ≥5% absolute delta to justify the double-call cost.
